question: A student is editing an English essay and decides to format a five-line prose excerpt as a block quotation. To conform to MLA guidelines, the student must start the quotation on a new line, indent the entire excerpt half an inch from the left margin, and remove the enclosing ______.
answer_explanation: According to the MLA Handbook, when a prose quotation runs more than four lines, it must be formatted as a block quotation. This editing process involves starting the quote on a new line, indenting the entire text half an inch from the left margin, and omitting the enclosing quotation marks, because the block formatting itself already indicates that the text is a direct quote.
subject: ELA
